subject: [RFC PATCH 09/45] mm: page_alloc: introduce superpageblock metadata for 1GB anti-fragmentation

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch introduces a new data structure called superpageblock to track pageblock composition at a coarser granularity, specifically targeting 1GB anti-fragmentation. The superpageblock groups pageblocks and maintains counts of pageblocks by migratetype, as well as a list_head for future organization by fullness category. This is done to enable steering of unmovable/reclaimable allocations into already-tainted superpageblocks and preserve clean superpageblocks for 1GB hugepage allocation. The patch adds new fields to the mmzone.h header file and modifies several other files to implement this new data structure, with no changes to allocation behavior.
